volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 1.1100 - Section 1201)

sections.label: Section 1.1100
sections.original: δάφναν τʼ εὐερνέα καὶ
sections.translation: and the well-grown laurel and the holy shoot of gray-green olive , Leto ’ s dear child , and the lake that rolls about its ripples , where the melodious swan

sections.label: Section 1.1105
sections.original: δὸς Μούσας θεραπεύει .
sections.translation: serves the Muses .

sections.label: Section 1.1106
sections.original: ὦ πολλαὶ δακρύων λιβάδες ,
sections.translation: O streams of tears that fell onto my cheeks , when my city was destroyed and the enemy forced me to sail ,

sections.label: Section 1.1110
sections.original: πολεμίων ἐρετμοῖσι καὶ λόγχαις .
sections.translation: by their oars , by their spears ! Purchased by gold , I came to a barbarian home , where I serve Agamemnon ’ s daughter ,

sections.label: Section 1.1115
sections.original: παῖδʼ Ἀγαμεμνονίαν λατρεύ-
sections.translation: the attendant maid of the deer-killing goddess , and the altars where no sheep are sacrificed ; and I envy ruin that is wretched throughout , for when you are brought up in harsh necessity , you do not suffer .

sections.label: Section 1.1120
sections.original: μεταβάλλει δυσδαιμονία ·
sections.translation: Misery changes ; life is hard for mortals , when they are treated badly after happiness .

sections.label: Section 2.1123
sections.original: καὶ σὲ μέν , πότνιʼ , Ἀργεία
sections.translation: And you , lady , the Argive penteconter will bear you home ;

sections.label: Section 2.1125
sections.original: συρίζων θʼ ὁ κηροδέτας
sections.translation: the wax-bound reed of the mountain god Pan , piping , will shout to the oars , and Phoebus the prophet , with the ring of his seven-stringed lyre ,

sections.label: Section 2.1130
sections.original: ἀείδων ἄξει λιπαρὰν
sections.translation: singing , will guide you well to the gleaming land of the Athenians . Leaving me here , you will go with splashing oars . In the breeze , the forestays of the ship that carries you swiftly

sections.label: Section 2.1135
sections.original: πὲρ στόλον ἐκπετάσουσι πόδα
sections.translation: will spread out over the front beyond the prow .

sections.label: Section 2.1138
sections.original: λαμπροὺς ἱπποδρόμους βαίην ,
sections.translation: May I come to the bright race-course , where the sun ’ s fire goes ;

sections.label: Section 2.1140
sections.original: οἰκείων δʼ ὑπὲρ θαλάμων
sections.translation: over the chambers of my home , may I cease to flutter the wings on my back . May I take my stand in the dances of glorious marriages , where I stood as a maiden ,

sections.label: Section 2.1145
sections.original: παρὰ πόδʼ εἱλίσσουσα φίλας
sections.translation: twirling about in the dancing bands of other girls , away from my dear mother ; rushing on to the contest of charms , the luxuriant strife of hair , I covered my cheeks with the multi-colored veil

sections.label: Section 2.1150
sections.original: καὶ πλοκάμους περιβαλλομένα
sections.translation: and shadowed them with the locks of my hair .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original: ποῦ ʼσθʼ ἡ πυλωρὸς τῶνδε δωμάτων γυνὴ
sections.translation: Where is the gate-keeper of this temple , the woman of Hellas ? Has she already begun the rites on the strangers ?

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: ἀδύτοις ἐν ἁγνοῖς σῶμα λάμπονται πυρί ;
sections.translation: Are they glowing with fire in the holy sanctuary ?

sections.label: Section 1156
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἐστίν , ἥ σοι πάντʼ , ἄναξ , ἐρεῖ σαφῶς .
sections.translation: Here she is , to tell you everything clearly , lord .

sections.label: Section 1157
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Oh ! Daughter of Agamemnon , why have you lifted up in your arms the statue of the goddess from its pedestal that must not be moved ?

sections.label: Section 1159
sections.original: ἄναξ , ἔχʼ αὐτοῦ πόδα σὸν ἐν παραστάσιν .
sections.translation: Lord , stand there in the entrance !

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , Ἰφιγένεια , καινὸν ἐν δόμοις ;
sections.translation: Iphigenia , what has happened in the temple ?

sections.label: Section 1161
sections.original: ἀπέπτυσʼ · Ὁσίᾳ γὰρ δίδωμʼ ἔπος τόδε .
sections.translation: I spit out the pollution ; ( turning to Thoas to explain .) I say this for Holiness .

sections.label: Section 1162
sections.original: τί φροιμιάζῃ νεοχμόν ; ἐξαύδα σαφῶς .
sections.translation: What is this news in your introduction ? Tell it clearly .

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original: οὐ καθαρά μοι τὰ θύματʼ ἠγρεύσασθʼ , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: The victims you caught for me are not pure , lord .

sections.label: Section 1164
sections.original: τί τοὐκδιδάξαν τοῦτό σʼ ; ἢ δόξαν λέγεις ;
sections.translation: What taught you this ? Or are you only saying what you think ?

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: βρέτας τὸ τῆς θεοῦ πάλιν ἕδρας ἀπεστράφη .
sections.translation: The image of the goddess turned back from its place .

sections.label: Section 1166
sections.original: αὐτόματον , ἤ νιν σεισμὸς ἔστρεψε χθονός ;
sections.translation: By itself , or did an earthquake turn it ?

sections.label: Section 1167
sections.original: αὐτόματον · ὄψιν δʼ ὀμμάτων ξυνήρμοσεν .
sections.translation: By itself ; it closed up its eyes .

sections.label: Section 1168
sections.original: ἡ δʼ αἰτία τίς ; ἦ τὸ τῶν ξένων μύσος ;
sections.translation: What was the reason ? Was it the uncleanness of the strangers ?

sections.label: Section 1169
sections.original: ἥδʼ , οὐδὲν ἄλλο · δεινὰ γὰρ δεδράκατον .
sections.translation: That was the reason , and nothing else ; for they have done dreadful things .

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ τινʼ ἔκανον βαρβάρων ἀκτῆς ἔπι ;
sections.translation: What , have they killed one of the barbarians on the shore ?

sections.label: Section 1171
sections.original: οἰκεῖον ἦλθον τὸν φόνον κεκτημένοι .
sections.translation: They come with murder done at home .

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.original: τίνʼ ; εἰς ἔρον γὰρ τοῦ μαθεῖν πεπτώκαμεν .
sections.translation: What murder ? For I strongly want to know .

sections.label: Section 1173
sections.original: μητέρα κατειργάσαντο κοινωνῷ ξίφει .
sections.translation: They killed their mother together with their swords .

sections.label: Section 1174
sections.original: Ἄπολλον , οὐδʼ ἐν βαρβάροις ἔτλη τις ἄν .
sections.translation: Apollo ! No barbarian would have dared this .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: πάσης διωγμοῖς ἠλάθησαν Ἑλλάδος .
sections.translation: They were driven in pursuit from all of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1176
sections.original: ἦ τῶνδʼ ἕκατι δῆτʼ ἄγαλμʼ ἔξω φέρεις ;
sections.translation: Is it for this that you bring the statue outside ?

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: σεμνόν γʼ ὑπʼ αἰθέρʼ , ὡς μεταστήσω φόνου .
sections.translation: Yes , to the holy air , to remove it from slaughter .

sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: μίασμα δʼ ἔγνως τοῖν ξένοιν ποίῳ τρόπῳ ;
sections.translation: How did you know the blood-pollution of the strangers ?

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: ἤλεγχον , ὡς θεᾶς βρέτας ἀπεστράφη πάλιν .
sections.translation: I questioned them , because the image of the goddess had turned back .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: σοφήν σʼ ἔθρεψεν Ἑλλάς , ὡς ᾔσθου καλῶς .
sections.translation: Hellas has brought you up to be clever ; how well you understood this .

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: καὶ μὴν καθεῖσαν δέλεαρ ἡδύ μοι φρενῶν .
sections.translation: And yet they dangled a sweet lure for my heart .

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original: τῶν Ἀργόθεν τι φίλτρον ἀγγέλλοντέ σοι ;
sections.translation: Reporting to you some charm of news from Argos ?

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.original: τὸν μόνον Ὀρέστην ἐμὸν ἀδελφὸν εὐτυχεῖν .
sections.translation: That Orestes , my only brother , is happy .

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: ὡς δή σφε σῴσαις ἡδοναῖς ἀγγελμάτων .
sections.translation: So that you might save them , for the delight of their messages .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: καὶ πατέρα γε ζῆν καὶ καλῶς πράσσειν ἐμόν .
sections.translation: And that my father is alive and doing well .

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐς τὸ τῆς θεοῦ γʼ ἐξένευσας εἰκότως .
sections.translation: But you turned to the goddess , with reason .

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: πᾶσάν γε μισοῦσʼ Ἑλλάδʼ , ἥ μʼ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Yes , for I hate all Hellas , which has ruined me .

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: τὸν νόμον ἀνάγκη τὸν προκείμενον σέβειν .
sections.translation: We must reverence the law as it stands .

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐν ἔργῳ χέρνιβες ξίφος τε σόν ;
sections.translation: But the libations and your sword are not at work ?

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.original: ἁγνοῖς καθαρμοῖς πρῶτά νιν νίψαι θέλω .
sections.translation: First I want to wash them , with holy purification .

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: πηγαῖσιν ὑδάτων ἢ θαλασσίᾳ δρόσῳ ;
sections.translation: In fountain waters , or the drops of the sea ?

sections.label: Section 1193
sections.original: θάλασσα κλύζει πάντα τἀνθρώπων κακά .
sections.translation: The sea washes away all men ’ s evils .

sections.label: Section 1194
sections.original: ὁσιώτερον γοῦν τῇ θεῷ πέσοιεν ἄν .
sections.translation: They would certainly be holier victims for the goddess .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: καὶ τἀμά γʼ οὕτω μᾶλλον ἂν καλῶς ἔχοι .
sections.translation: And in this way my plans would succeed better .

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original: οὔκουν πρὸς αὐτὸν ναὸν ἐκπίπτει κλύδων ;
sections.translation: Doesn ’ t the wave beat against this very temple ?

sections.label: Section 1197
sections.original: ἐρημίας δεῖ · καὶ γὰρ ἄλλα δράσομεν .
sections.translation: This requires solitude ; and I shall do more .

sections.label: Section 1198
sections.original: ἄγʼ ἔνθα χρῄζεις · οὐ φιλῶ τἄρρηθʼ ὁρᾶν .
sections.translation: Then where you wish ; I do not want to see what should not be seen .

sections.label: Section 1199
sections.original: ἁγνιστέον μοι καὶ τὸ τῆς θεοῦ βρέτας .
sections.translation: I must purify the image of the goddess also .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: εἴπερ γε κηλὶς ἔβαλέ νιν μητροκτόνος .
sections.translation: Yes , if a stain from the matricide has fallen on it .

sections.label: Section 1201
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτʼ ἄν νιν ἠράμην βάθρων ἄπο .
sections.translation: For I would not have lifted it from its base otherwise .
